---
title: "Production Quality Python Packages"
subtitle: ": CI/CD, PyPI, and the Poster Showcase"
date: 05/20/2025
date-format: long
author:
  - name: Hyesop Shin
    orcid: 0000-0003-2637-7933
    email: hyesop.shin@auckland.ac.nz
    affiliations:
      - name: University of Auckland
        address: 23 Symonds Street
        city: Auckland
        postal-code: 1010
format: 
  revealjs:
      logo: Figs/uoa-logo.png
      css: logo.css
      slide-number: true
      show-slide-number: print
editor: source
include-in-header:
  - text: |
      <style type="text/css">
      ul li ul li {
        font-size: 0.7em;
      }
      </style>
---

## Recap of Previous Lectures

* Testing with pytest
* Documentation with docstrings
* Publishing to TestPyPI
* Week 10 deliverable (5%)

## Today's Lecture

::: {.incremental}
* Complete package walkthrough
* **<span style="color:blue">CI/CD</span>** with GitHub Actions
* Publishing to **<span style="color:blue">real PyPI</span>**
* **<span style="color:blue">Poster design</span>** for showcase
* Assignment 3 final requirements
:::

## Assignment 3 Timeline

::: {style="font-size: 90%;"}
**<span style="color:blue">Week 11 (This week)</span>**:
- Polish your package
- Set up CI/CD (optional)
- Design poster
- Optional: Publish to real PyPI

**<span style="color:blue">Week 12 (Next week)</span>**:
- **<span style="color:red">Thursday 5 June: Assignment 3 due (30%)</span>**
- **<span style="color:red">Thursday 5 June: Poster showcase (10%)</span>**
- Total: **<span style="color:red">40% of final grade</span>**
:::

# Part 1: Complete Package Example

## Auckland GIS Toolkit

**<span style="color:blue">Real-world package structure</span>**

::: {style="font-size: 85%;"}
```
auckland-gis/
├── .github/
│   └── workflows/
│       └── test.yml           # CI/CD
├── src/
│   └── auckland_gis/
│       ├── __init__.py
│       ├── data.py            # Data loading
│       ├── metrics.py         # Calculations
│       └── viz.py             # Visualizations
├── tests/
│   ├── test_data.py
│   ├── test_metrics.py
│   └── test_viz.py
├── examples/
│   └── usage_example.ipynb
├── LICENSE
├── pyproject.toml
└── README.md
```
:::

## Key Functions Overview

::: {style="font-size: 90%;"}
**<span style="color:blue">data.py</span>**
- `load_sa2()` - Load Auckland boundaries
- `validate_join()` - Safe spatial joins

**<span style="color:blue">metrics.py</span>**
- `calculate_density()` - Population density
- `calculate_accessibility()` - Proximity analysis

**<span style="color:blue">viz.py</span>**
- `create_choropleth()` - Standard maps
:::

## Function 1: Load SA2 with Validation

::: {style="font-size: 70%;"}
```python
# src/auckland_gis/data.py
import geopandas as gpd
from pathlib import Path
import warnings

def load_sa2(path=None, validate=True):
    """
    Load Auckland SA2 boundaries.
    
    Parameters
    ----------
    path : str, optional
        Path to SA2 file. If None, downloads from repository.
    validate : bool, default True
        Whether to validate CRS and geometry
    
    Returns
    -------
    GeoDataFrame
        SA2 boundaries in NZTM (EPSG:2193)
    """
    if path is None:
        path = _download_sa2_boundaries()
    
    gdf = gpd.read_file(path)
    
    if validate:
        gdf = _validate_sa2(gdf)
    
    return gdf

def _validate_sa2(gdf):
    """Validate SA2 GeoDataFrame."""
    # Check CRS
    if gdf.crs != 'EPSG:2193':
        warnings.warn(f"Converting from {gdf.crs} to EPSG:2193")
        gdf = gdf.to_crs('EPSG:2193')
    
    # Check required columns
    required = ['SA2_code', 'SA2_name', 'geometry']
    missing = set(required) - set(gdf.columns)
    if missing:
        raise ValueError(f"Missing columns: {missing}")
    
    return gdf
```
:::

## Function 2: Safe Joins with Reporting

::: {style="font-size: 70%;"}
```python
def validate_join(gdf, df, key, how='left'):
    """
    Join DataFrames with validation reporting.
    
    Returns
    -------
    result : GeoDataFrame
        Joined GeoDataFrame
    report : dict
        Validation report with match statistics
    """
    # Check for duplicates
    duplicates = df[key].duplicated()
    if duplicates.any():
        dup_keys = df.loc[duplicates, key].tolist()
        raise ValueError(f"Duplicate keys: {dup_keys[:5]}")
    
    # Store original keys
    left_keys = set(gdf[key].unique())
    right_keys = set(df[key].unique())
    
    # Perform join
    result = gdf.merge(df, on=key, how=how)
    
    # Generate report
    unmatched = left_keys - right_keys
    report = {
        'all_matched': len(unmatched) == 0,
        'unmatched_count': len(unmatched),
        'unmatched_keys': sorted(list(unmatched)),
    }
    
    return result, report
```
:::

## Example Usage

::: {style="font-size: 85%;"}
```python
from auckland_gis import (
    load_sa2,
    validate_join,
    calculate_density,
    create_choropleth
)
import pandas as pd

# Load boundaries
sa2 = load_sa2()

# Load census
census = pd.read_csv('census_2023.csv')

# Safe join with reporting
result, report = validate_join(sa2, census, key='SA2_code')

if not report['all_matched']:
    print(f"⚠️  {report['unmatched_count']} zones missing data")

# Calculate density
result = calculate_density(result)

# Create map
fig, ax = create_choropleth(result, column='density')
```
:::

# Part 2: CI/CD with GitHub Actions

## What is CI/CD?

<div id="contentframe" style="position:absolute; top: 250px; left: 50px;">
<iframe src="https://giphy.com/embed/3oKIPnAiaMCws8nOsE" width="350" height="250" frameBorder="0" class="giphy-embed" allowFullScreen></iframe>
</div>

::: {style="position:absolute; top: 280px; left: 450px;"}
**<span style="color:blue">CI/CD =</span>**

**<span style="color:blue">Continuous Integration</span>**
- Automatically test code

**<span style="color:blue">Continuous Deployment</span>**
- Automatically deploy

**<span style="color:blue">Benefits:</span>**
- Catch bugs early
- Ensure quality
- Automate boring tasks
:::

## GitHub Actions Workflow

**<span style="color:blue">Create</span>** `.github/workflows/test.yml`:

::: {style="font-size: 70%;"}
```yaml
name: Tests

on:
  push:
    branches: [ main, develop ]
  pull_request:
    branches: [ main ]

jobs:
  test:
    runs-on: ${{ matrix.os }}
    strategy:
      matrix:
        os: [ubuntu-latest, windows-latest, macos-latest]
        python-version: ['3.10', '3.11', '3.12']
    
    steps:
    - uses: actions/checkout@v4
    
    - name: Set up Python
      uses: actions/setup-python@v5
      with:
        python-version: ${{ matrix.python-version }}
    
    - name: Install uv
      run: pip install uv
    
    - name: Install dependencies
      run: uv pip install -e ".[dev]"
    
    - name: Run tests
      run: uv run pytest --cov=auckland_gis
    
    - name: Upload coverage
      uses: codecov/codecov-action@v4
```
:::

## What This Does

::: {.incremental}
* **<span style="color:blue">Runs on every push</span>** to main/develop
* **<span style="color:blue">Tests on 3 operating systems</span>** (Ubuntu, Windows, macOS)
* **<span style="color:blue">Tests on 3 Python versions</span>** (3.10, 3.11, 3.12)
* **<span style="color:blue">Runs all tests</span>** automatically
* **<span style="color:blue">Reports coverage</span>** to Codecov
* **<span style="color:blue">Shows status badge</span>** on GitHub
:::

## Status Badges

**<span style="color:blue">Add to your README.md:</span>**

::: {style="font-size: 90%;"}
```markdown
# Auckland GIS Toolkit

[![Tests](https://github.com/yourusername/auckland-gis/workflows/Tests/badge.svg)](https://github.com/yourusername/auckland-gis/actions)
[![codecov](https://codecov.io/gh/yourusername/auckland-gis/branch/main/graph/badge.svg)](https://codecov.io/gh/yourusername/auckland-gis)
[![PyPI version](https://badge.fury.io/py/auckland-gis.svg)](https://pypi.org/project/auckland-gis/)

Reusable utilities for Auckland urban analytics.
```
:::

![](https://github.com/geopandas/geopandas/raw/main/doc/source/_static/geopandas_logo.png){fig-align=center width=40%}

## Pre-commit Hooks

**<span style="color:blue">Automatic code formatting</span>**

::: {style="font-size: 85%;"}
```yaml
# .pre-commit-config.yaml
repos:
  - repo: https://github.com/psf/black
    rev: 23.12.1
    hooks:
      - id: black

  - repo: https://github.com/astral-sh/ruff-pre-commit
    rev: v0.1.13
    hooks:
      - id: ruff
        args: [--fix]

  - repo: https://github.com/pre-commit/pre-commit-hooks
    rev: v4.5.0
    hooks:
      - id: trailing-whitespace
      - id: end-of-file-fixer
```

**Install:**
```bash
pip install pre-commit
pre-commit install
```
:::

## Your Turn: Set Up CI/CD

::: {style="font-size: 110%;"}
**<span style="color:blue">Task</span>** (Optional but encouraged):

1. Create `.github/workflows/test.yml`
2. Copy the workflow code
3. Push to GitHub
4. Check Actions tab
5. Add badge to README

**Time**: 15 minutes
:::

# Part 3: Publishing to Real PyPI

## TestPyPI → Real PyPI

::: {style="font-size: 90%;"}
**<span style="color:blue">You've practiced with TestPyPI</span>**

Now it's time for the **<span style="color:red">real thing</span>**!

::: {.incremental}
* **Permanent** - Can't delete
* **Visible to everyone**
* **Anyone can install**
* **Part of your portfolio**
* **Shows on your CV!**
:::
:::

## Pre-Publication Checklist {.smaller}

::: {style="font-size: 80%;"}
**Code:**
- [ ] All tests passing
- [ ] Coverage >80%
- [ ] No linting errors

**Documentation:**
- [ ] README complete
- [ ] All functions documented
- [ ] Examples working
- [ ] CHANGELOG updated

**Metadata:**
- [ ] Version number correct (≥1.0.0)
- [ ] Dependencies accurate
- [ ] License file present
- [ ] Classifiers appropriate

**Testing:**
- [ ] Tested in fresh environment
- [ ] All examples work
:::

## Create PyPI Account

::: {.incremental}
1. Visit **<span style="color:blue">https://pypi.org/account/register/</span>**
2. Use professional email
3. **<span style="color:red">Enable 2FA</span>** (required!)
4. Generate API token
5. Scope: Project-specific (safer)
:::

## Publishing to PyPI

::: {style="font-size: 90%;"}
```bash
# 1. Clean old builds
rm -rf dist/

# 2. Check version is unique
grep "version" pyproject.toml

# 3. Run tests one last time
uv run pytest

# 4. Build
uv build

# 5. Publish to real PyPI
export UV_PUBLISH_TOKEN=pypi-YOUR_TOKEN
uv publish

# ✓ Successfully published to PyPI!
```
:::

## Semantic Versioning

**<span style="color:blue">Format</span>**: `MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH`

::: {style="font-size: 90%;"}
```
1.2.3
│ │ └─ Patch: Bug fixes
│ └─── Minor: New features
└───── Major: Breaking changes
```

**Examples:**
- `0.1.0` → `0.1.1`: Fixed bug
- `0.1.1` → `0.2.0`: Added feature
- `0.2.0` → `1.0.0`: Changed API (breaking)
:::

## Maintaining Your Package

::: {.incremental}
* **<span style="color:blue">Respond to issues</span>** - Help users
* **<span style="color:blue">Update dependencies</span>** - Keep secure
* **<span style="color:blue">Fix bugs</span>** - Release patches
* **<span style="color:blue">Add features</span>** - Minor versions
* **<span style="color:blue">Document changes</span>** - CHANGELOG.md
:::

# Part 4: Poster Showcase

## Poster Requirements

**<span style="color:red">Due: Thursday 5 June (10% of grade)</span>**

::: {style="font-size: 90%;"}
**Format:**
- **<span style="color:blue">A1 size</span>** (594mm × 841mm)
- **<span style="color:blue">Portrait</span>** orientation
- **<span style="color:blue">PDF</span>** for printing

**Content:**
- Package name and purpose
- Key features
- Code examples
- Visual outputs
- PyPI URL + QR code
- Your contact info
:::

## Poster Layout Example {.smaller}

::: {style="font-size: 75%;"}
```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  Micromobility Analytics Toolkit        │ ← Big title
│  Your Name - University of Auckland     │
├─────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ THE PROBLEM                             │
│ • E-scooters lack analysis tools        │
│ • Trip data is messy                    │
│ • Need standardized metrics             │
├────────────────┬────────────────────────┤
│  KEY FEATURES  │   CODE EXAMPLE         │
│ • Trip process │ from micromobility...  │
│ • Geofencing   │ trips = process_...    │
│ • OD flows     │ flows = od_flows...    │
├────────────────┴────────────────────────┤
│  [Large visualization - map/chart]      │
│                                          │
├──────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ APPLICATIONS        LINKS                │
│ • Urban planning   [QR to PyPI]         │
│ • Policy eval      your.email@...       │
└──────────────────────────────────────────┘
```
:::

## Design Tips

::: {.incremental}
* **<span style="color:blue">Large fonts</span>** - Readable from 2m (titles: 72pt+)
* **<span style="color:blue">High contrast</span>** - Dark text on light background
* **<span style="color:blue">White space</span>** - Don't cram everything in
* **<span style="color:blue">One main message</span>** - What's the key point?
* **<span style="color:blue">Visual hierarchy</span>** - Guide the eye
:::

## QR Code for PyPI

**<span style="color:blue">Generate at</span>**: qr-code-generator.com

![](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/d/d0/QR_code_for_mobile_English_Wikipedia.svg/330px-QR_code_for_mobile_English_Wikipedia.svg.png){fig-align=center width=30%}

**<span style="color:blue">Links to your PyPI package!</span>**

## 2-Minute Pitch

::: {style="font-size: 90%;"}
**<span style="color:blue">Structure</span>**:

1. **Hook** (15s) - Problem statement
2. **Problem** (15s) - Why it matters
3. **Solution** (30s) - Your package
4. **Demo** (45s) - Code + results
5. **Impact** (15s) - Who benefits

**Tips:**
- Practice timing
- Don't read from poster
- Make eye contact
- Show enthusiasm!
:::

## Printing Your Poster

::: {style="font-size: 90%;"}
**<span style="color:blue">University Print Centre</span>**:
- Website: printcentre.auckland.ac.nz
- Cost: ~$25-30 for A1 colour
- Turnaround: 24-48 hours
- **<span style="color:red">Print by: 4 June</span>**

**Backup**: Bring PDF on USB to showcase
:::

## Your Turn: Start Poster Design

::: {style="font-size: 110%;"}
**<span style="color:blue">Task</span>**: Create poster outline

1. Choose package topic
2. List key features (3-5)
3. Select best visualization
4. Draft main message
5. Sketch layout on paper

**Time**: 20 minutes
:::

# Assignment 3 Requirements

## What to Submit (30% of Grade)

**<span style="color:red">Due: Thursday 5 June, 5pm</span>**

::: {style="font-size: 85%;"}
**1. Published to PyPI**
- Accessible at pypi.org
- Installable via `pip install`

**2. GitHub Repository**
- Complete src/ layout
- Tests (>80% coverage)
- Professional README
- MIT or similar license

**3. Documentation**
- Docstrings for all functions
- Installation guide
- Usage examples
- API reference

**4. Example Notebook**
- Demonstrates key features
- Real urban analytics
- Clear, reproducible
:::

---

### What to Submit (continued)

::: {style="font-size: 85%;"}
**5. Brief Report** (1-2 pages)
- Package functionality
- Design decisions
- Potential applications
- Challenges and solutions

**Plus: Poster Showcase (10%)**
- A1 poster printed
- 2-minute pitch prepared
:::

## Evaluation Rubric {.smaller}

::: {style="font-size: 75%;"}
| Criterion | Weight | Excellent | Good | Adequate |
|-----------|--------|-----------|------|----------|
| **Code Quality** | 10% | Clean, organized, PEP 8 | Minor issues | Messy |
| **Documentation** | 10% | Comprehensive | Complete | Basic |
| **Testing** | 8% | >90% coverage | >80% coverage | >70% |
| **Publication** | 2% | Works perfectly | Minor issues | Published |

**Total Assignment 3: 30%**
**Poster Showcase: 10%**
**Combined: 40% of final grade**
:::

## Package Ideas

::: {style="font-size: 90%;"}
**Recommended:**
- **<span style="color:blue">Micromobility toolkit</span>** - E-scooter analysis
- **<span style="color:blue">Accessibility calculator</span>** - 15-minute city
- **<span style="color:blue">Pedestrian analytics</span>** - Flow analysis
- **<span style="color:blue">Network utilities</span>** - Street analysis
- **<span style="color:blue">Urban form metrics</span>** - Morphology

**Build on your assignments!**
:::

## Timeline for Success

::: {style="font-size: 85%;"}
**Week 11 (This week):**
- [ ] Finalize package code
- [ ] Add all tests (>80%)
- [ ] Write documentation
- [ ] Design poster
- [ ] Optional: Publish to real PyPI

**Week 12 (Next week):**
- [ ] Final testing
- [ ] Print poster
- [ ] Practice pitch
- [ ] Submit everything
- [ ] Showcase presentation!
:::

## Summary

::: {.incremental}
* Complete package examples
* CI/CD with GitHub Actions
* Publishing to real PyPI
* Poster design and showcase
* Assignment 3 + Poster = 40%
:::

## Next Week

**<span style="color:blue">Week 12: Final Week</span>**

::: {.incremental}
* King's Birthday (public holiday)
* Labs operate normally
* Assignment 3 due Thursday
* **<span style="color:red">Poster Showcase Thursday!</span>**
* Celebrate your achievements! 🎉
:::

## Tips for Final Week

::: {.incremental}
* **<span style="color:blue">Start poster early</span>** - Don't wait!
* **<span style="color:blue">Test in fresh environment</span>** - Does it install?
* **<span style="color:blue">Practice your pitch</span>** - Time yourself
* **<span style="color:blue">Help each other</span>** - Review each other's work
* **<span style="color:blue">Be proud!</span>** - You're publishing real software
:::

## Resources

* GitHub Actions: docs.github.com/actions
* PyPI Help: pypi.org/help
* Semantic Versioning: semver.org
* Poster design: canva.com (free templates)

## You're Almost There!

<div id="contentframe" style="position:absolute; top: 250px; left: 200px;">
<iframe src="https://giphy.com/embed/3o7abKhOpu0NwenH3O" width="400" height="300" frameBorder="0" class="giphy-embed" allowFullScreen></iframe>
</div>

::: {style="position:absolute; top: 580px; left: 100px; font-size: 120%;"}
**<span style="color:blue">One more week until you're published Python package authors!</span>**
:::

## Thanks! <br> Q & A {style="text-align: center;"}

**See you next week for the showcase!**

<div style="width:840px"><iframe allow="fullscreen" frameBorder="0" height="480" src="https://giphy.com/embed/g9582DNuQppxC/video" width="840"></iframe></div>
